issues.title: Pathway side panel doesn't push history
issues.description: On /pathway/:channelUrl, opening Alternative Plan side panel and pressing browser back navigates out of the entire pathway board (returns to /pathway) instead of closing the panel. User loses scroll position and board context.
issues.fix: When opening the panel, push a hash like #alt-plan and listen for popstate to close. Or wrap in MUI Drawer with native ESC + history integration.
issues.dev_prompt:
Goal: make the Alternative Plan side panel on /pathway/:channelUrl close on browser back instead of dismissing the entire pathway board.

Files to edit:
- apps/called-chat/src/views/Community/Pathway/PathwayBoard/AlternativePlanPanel/AlternativePlanPanel.tsx
- The Zustand store that exposes isAlternativePlanOpen / setIsAlternativePlanOpen (locate via grep on isAlternativePlanOpen in apps/called-chat/src/stores).

Changes:
1. In AlternativePlanPanel.tsx, add a useEffect that runs when isAlternativePlanOpen flips true: call window.history.pushState({ altPlan: true }, '', `${window.location.pathname}${window.location.search}#alt-plan`).
2. In the same effect, register a popstate listener handlePopState that closes the panel (setIsAlternativePlanOpen(false)). Return a cleanup that removes the listener and, if the hash still equals #alt-plan and the panel is closing via UI, calls history.back() once to keep history clean.
3. Add Escape key handling (handleKeyDown) for parity.
4. Use the handle* prefix per project convention; do not use inline lambdas in JSX.

Acceptance criteria:
- Opening the panel adds one history entry; closing via the X button or backdrop removes it.
- Browser back closes the panel and keeps the user on /pathway/:channelUrl with scroll preserved.
- Pressing Escape closes the panel.
- No console errors on rapid open/close.
- No regression for users who deep-link to /pathway/:channelUrl#alt-plan (panel should open).
issues.notion_ticket_prompt:
/notion-ticket
Title: Pathway alternative plan panel should close on browser back
Team: web-isidore
Sprint: current sprint

Story:
As a user viewing a pathway board with the Alternative Plan side panel open
I want the browser back button to dismiss just the panel
so that I don't lose my scroll position and board context.

Tasks:
- Push a history entry (hash #alt-plan) when the panel opens in AlternativePlanPanel.tsx
- Add a popstate listener that closes the panel when the user goes back
- Wire Escape key to the same close handler for keyboard parity
- Make sure closing via the X or backdrop also unwinds the history entry
- Verify deep links to /pathway/:channelUrl#alt-plan still open the panel

Acceptance Criteria:
- Opening the panel pushes exactly one history entry
- Browser back closes the panel only, board stays mounted with scroll preserved
- Escape closes the panel
- Closing via UI controls leaves history clean (no orphaned #alt-plan entry)
- No console errors on rapid open/close cycles

issues.title: All modals dismiss the page when user presses browser back
issues.description: Every modal (QR code, Edit Community, Create Channel wizard, Delete confirmations, Add Members, etc.) uses Zustand boolean state with no history push. Pressing browser back / Android system back navigates away from the underlying page entirely instead of just closing the modal.
issues.fix: Add pushState({modal: key}) on open, listen for popstate to dismiss. Or migrate to a route-as-modal pattern (?modal=qr). Touches ModalController + ~12 modal call-sites.
issues.dev_prompt:
Goal: make every app modal closeable via browser/system back without unmounting the underlying page.

Files to edit:
- apps/called-chat/src/components/Modals/ModalController.tsx (primary)
- Any modal call-sites that bypass ModalController (search for setIsOpen pattern in apps/called-chat/src/components/Modals/* and views/**/*Modal*.tsx).

Changes:
1. In ModalController, when a modal opens (state transitions from null/closed to a modal key), call window.history.pushState({ modal: key }, '', window.location.href).
2. Add a single popstate listener at the controller level: handlePopState reads event.state, and if there is no { modal } stamp and a modal is open, close it via the existing close action.
3. When a modal closes via UI (close button, backdrop, ESC, success action), check if window.history.state?.modal === key and call window.history.back() to pop the synthetic entry. Guard against double-pop.
4. Expose a small hook useModalHistory(key, isOpen, onClose) so ad-hoc modals (not in ModalController) can opt in with a one-liner.
5. Use handle* prefix; no inline lambdas.

Acceptance criteria:
- Opening any modal (QR code, Edit Community, Create Channel wizard, Delete confirmations, Add Members) adds one history entry.
- Browser/system back closes the modal and keeps the underlying route mounted.
- Closing via UI controls leaves the history stack clean (no leftover synthetic entries).
- Android system back gesture closes a single modal per press, not the page.
- Nested modal-on-modal still resolves one layer per back press.
issues.notion_ticket_prompt:
/notion-ticket
Title: Modals should close on browser/system back, not navigate away
Team: web-isidore
Sprint: current sprint

Story:

I want browser/system back to close just the modal
so that I don't get bounced off the page I was working on.

- Pop the synthetic entry when a modal closes via UI controls
- Add a useModalHistory hook for modals that live outside ModalController
- Sweep modal call-sites (QR, Edit Community, Create Channel, Delete confirms, Add Members) and adopt the hook where needed

Acceptance Criteria:

- Android back gesture closes one modal per press
- Nested modals unwind one layer at a time

issues.title: Trailing-slash inconsistency in delete handlers
issues.description: One code path navigates to /communities/{id}/ (with trailing slash), another to /communities (no slash) within the same component. Wouter may treat these as different URLs; users can end up with duplicate-history entries.
issues.fix: Always use /communities/${id} (no trailing slash) via a buildUrl helper. Add a normaliser in beforeNav().
issues.dev_prompt:
Goal: normalize navigation URLs after community delete so both branches use the same trailing-slash style.

File to edit:
- apps/called-chat/src/views/Community/Communities/EntityDetails/EditDetails/DeleteEntityDialog/DeleteEntityDialog.tsx (lines 96 and 98)

Current state (verified):
- Line 96: setLocation(`/${calledChatRoutes.communities.route}/${affiliationToSwitchTo.channelUrl}/`) — trailing slash
- Line 98: setLocation(`/${calledChatRoutes.communities.route}`) — no trailing slash

Changes:
1. Drop the trailing slash on line 96 so it reads setLocation(`/${calledChatRoutes.communities.route}/${affiliationToSwitchTo.channelUrl}`).
2. Search the file for any other setLocation calls with a trailing slash and normalize them too.
3. Optional (do this only if time allows): add a tiny helper in apps/called-chat/src/utils/route.ts named buildCommunitiesPath(channelUrl?: string) that returns the canonical no-slash form, and use it here.

Acceptance criteria:
- Both branches of handleDeleteEntity navigate to URLs with no trailing slash.
- Deleting a community when other affiliations exist lands on /communities/{nextChannelUrl} (no slash).
- Deleting the last community lands on /communities (no slash).
- No duplicate history entries or visible URL flicker after delete.
- Existing tests still pass.
issues.notion_ticket_prompt:
/notion-ticket
Title: Normalize trailing slashes in community delete navigation
Team: web-isidore

As a user who just deleted or left a community
I want the resulting URL to be consistent (no trailing slash)

Tasks:
- Drop the trailing slash on DeleteEntityDialog.tsx line 96
- Audit the rest of DeleteEntityDialog for any other slash inconsistencies
- Optionally add a buildCommunitiesPath helper in utils/route.ts
- Re-run existing DeleteEntityDialog tests

Acceptance Criteria:

- /communities/{channelUrl} (no slash) when an affiliation remains
- /communities (no slash) when none remain
- No duplicate history entry after delete
- Existing tests pass

issues.title: Channel delete doesn't redirect on success
issues.description: After channel is deleted via the channel header ⋮ menu, the URL still points at /chat/{deletedChannelUrl}. Only React Query cache invalidation keeps the UI from showing stale data, but the URL is wrong. Refresh from this state lands on an empty channel.
issues.fix: After successful delete, setLocation('/chat/' + currentEntity.channelUrl) to fall back to the parent community chat, or '/chat' for a clean state.
issues.dev_prompt:
Goal: redirect the user away from a deleted channel's URL so refresh doesn't land them on an empty/404 state.

File to edit:
- apps/called-chat/src/views/Community/Chat/ChannelMenu/ChannelMenu.tsx (around lines 139-155, function handleDeleteChannelConfirmation)

Changes:
1. Import useTransitionLocation (or whatever the file uses for navigation — check existing imports) and destructure setLocation if not already available.
2. Determine the parent fallback URL. Prefer the current entity's primary channel: if currentEntity (or equivalent prop/hook) exposes channelUrl, use `/chat/${currentEntity.channelUrl}`. If not available, fall back to `/chat`.
3. Inside the deleteChannel onSuccess callback (currently invalidates v1/channel and v1/entity queries), append setLocation(fallbackUrl) AFTER the invalidations.
4. Keep query invalidation order: cache invalidation first so the new route doesn't render stale data.
5. Use handle* naming for any new helpers; no inline lambdas.

Acceptance criteria:
- Deleting a channel from the ⋮ menu navigates the user to the parent community chat (or /chat if none) before any 404 state can render.
- Refreshing immediately after delete does not show an empty channel shell at the old URL.
- Channel list updates to reflect the deletion.
- No console errors during the delete → redirect flow.
- Existing ChannelMenu tests still pass; add a test that asserts setLocation is called in onSuccess.
issues.notion_ticket_prompt:
/notion-ticket
Title: Redirect after channel delete so the URL doesn't point at a deleted channel
Team: web-isidore
Sprint: current sprint

Story:

so that I don't see an empty/broken channel shell on refresh.

Tasks:
- Read the current entity's channelUrl in ChannelMenu.tsx
- In the deleteChannel onSuccess callback, call setLocation to the parent or /chat
- Keep query invalidation first, navigation second
- Add a unit test that asserts setLocation runs in onSuccess

Acceptance Criteria:

- Refresh after delete does not render a stale empty channel
- Channel list reflects the deletion
- No console errors during delete
- Existing tests pass and the new test covers the redirect

issues.title: Post-create community routes to non-canonical /dashboard/
issues.description: Create Community wizard → Finish lands on /dashboard/ (with trailing slash) — a path that isn't the canonical home (/). Inconsistent with all other home navigations and creates a duplicate-feeling history entry.
issues.fix: Use '/' (or pin the user inside the new community at /groups/{newId}).
issues.dev_prompt:
Goal: stop routing to /dashboard/ (trailing slash) after community creation; use the canonical home path.

File to edit:
- apps/called-chat/src/views/Community/CommunityCreation/CommunityCreation.tsx (line 100)
- apps/called-chat/src/utils/route.ts (line 3 — home.route is currently 'dashboard')

Current state (verified):
- route.ts line 3: home: { route: 'dashboard', title: 'Home' }
- CommunityCreation.tsx line 100: setLocation(`/${calledChatRoutes.home.route}/`) → resolves to /dashboard/

Changes:
1. Decide canonical home. Per audit, the rest of the app treats / as home. Confirm by grepping setLocation calls in apps/called-chat/src for home references. If other call-sites use calledChatRoutes.home.route directly, prefer changing route.ts so home.route = '' and update the template to setLocation(`/${calledChatRoutes.home.route}`) yielding '/'.
2. If touching route.ts is too broad, the minimum fix is: change CommunityCreation.tsx line 100 to setLocation('/') and add a comment that home is the root.
3. Drop the trailing slash regardless.
4. Re-run lint and the CommunityCreation tests in apps/called-chat/src/views/Community/CommunityCreation/__tests__.

Acceptance criteria:
- Finishing the Create Community wizard navigates to / (or the chosen canonical home), never /dashboard/.
- No trailing-slash variant remains for the post-create redirect.
- Other places that use calledChatRoutes.home.route still resolve correctly (no broken nav).
- Existing CommunityCreation tests pass; update snapshots if any.
- Manual smoke: create a new community → land on home, header shows new community in the list.

issues.title: Headway iframe intercepts pointer events near top-right
issues.description: The #HW_frame_cont div covers the top-right region of the page even when the user is just browsing. Clicks on cards near the top-right (community cards, 'Show all communities') get swallowed silently — looks like the UI is broken. Automation has to forcibly remove it to interact with adjacent UI.
issues.fix: Make the Headway container pointer-events: none by default; set pointer-events: auto only on the dropdown contents when open. Or load Headway only on user intent (click the What's New badge).
issues.dev_prompt:
Goal: stop the Headway What's New iframe from swallowing clicks in the top-right of authenticated pages.

Files to edit:
- apps/called-chat/src/components/SiteHeader/HeadwayWidget.tsx
- apps/called-chat/src/components/SiteHeader/SiteHeader.module.scss

Current state (verified):
- HeadwayWidget renders <div id='headway-widget'> and Headway injects an iframe (#HW_frame_cont) that overlays the top-right region.
- SiteHeader.module.scss only styles .HW_badge_cont / .HW_badge today.

Changes:
1. In SiteHeader.module.scss inside the .headway block, add :global(#HW_frame_cont) { pointer-events: none; } as the default.
2. Add :global(#HW_frame_cont.HW_visible), :global(#HW_frame_cont[style*="display: block"]) { pointer-events: auto; } so the panel becomes interactive only when Headway opens it. Inspect Headway's actual class for the open state (likely HW_visible) via DevTools and pin the correct selector.
3. In HeadwayWidget.tsx, add a title to the iframe for a11y once it mounts: after Headway.init, use a MutationObserver on #headway-widget to set the iframe's title attribute to 'What\u2019s New from Called'. Clean up the observer on unmount.
4. Use handle* prefix for any new handlers. No inline lambdas in JSX.

Acceptance criteria:
- Clicking community cards or "Show all communities" near the top-right works on every authenticated page.
- Clicking the What's New trigger still opens the Headway panel and the panel is clickable.
- Closing the panel restores pointer-events: none on #HW_frame_cont.
- The injected iframe has a meaningful title attribute (axe stops flagging "frame has no accessible name").
- No regression to badge styling.
issues.notion_ticket_prompt:

issues.title: /profile/notifications is orphaned
issues.description: The route exists with 3 useful toggles (New Events / New Members / Join Requests), but no Settings sidebar link points to it. Reachable only via direct URL or the notification-sound pill on Edit Profile.
issues.fix: Add a 'Notifications' entry to the Settings sidebar between Blocked users and Give feedback.
issues.dev_prompt:
Goal: Make the orphaned `/profile/notifications` page reachable from the Settings sidebar on web (currently only rendered when `isMobileApp` is true).

File: `apps/called-chat/src/views/Profile/ProfileActions/index.tsx`.

Change: The `notificationSettings` link block (around lines 156-167) is wrapped in `{isMobileApp && (...)}`. Remove that gating so the Bell-icon Notifications row also renders on web. Place it directly under Blocked users and above the `nav_divider` so the order becomes: Edit Profile -> Manage communities (managePlan) -> Blocked users -> Notifications -> divider -> external rows. The route is already defined as `calledChatRoutes.notificationSettings` and the section component `NotificationSettings` is already wired up in `apps/called-chat/src/views/Profile/index.tsx`.

Acceptance criteria:
- Notifications row appears in the Settings sidebar on desktop web and on mobile web (not just inside the iOS/Android app).
- Clicking it routes to `/profile/notifications` and renders the existing toggles (New Events, New Members, Join Requests).
- Active-state styling (`styles.active`) works the same as siblings.
- No other rows change order or styling.
- Existing NotificationSettings tests still pass; lint is clean.

issues.title: Pathway has no CRUD on pathways themselves
issues.description: User sees 'Pathway' in nav. Clicks a card → board view. But there's no way to create or edit a pathway in the UI; pathways are server-side templates. Admins who want to set up a new flow have no self-serve path or even an explanation.
issues.fix: Small fix: add empty-state copy and a contact-support link explaining that pathways are configured by support. Larger fix (L): build full pathway CRUD UI.
issues.dev_prompt:
Goal (small variant): When an admin lands on `/pathway` with zero pathways, show an empty state explaining that pathways are configured by support, instead of an empty grid that looks broken.

File: `apps/called-chat/src/views/Community/Pathway/PathwayListing/PathwayListing.tsx`.

Change: After `usePathwayListing()` returns, branch when `!isLoading && items.length === 0` and render an empty-state block inside `<PageWrapper>` instead of the grid. Copy suggestion: heading `No pathways yet` and body `Pathways are set up by the Called team. Reach out to support and we will get one configured for your community.` Include a secondary action link to `calledLinks.supportLink` (open in new tab) using the existing Support link from `@/config`. Keep markup in line with the audit's plain-language rule (no jargon). Style with a new `empty_state` block in `PathwayListing.module.scss` using existing semantic tokens (`--text-default`, `--text-weak`, `--bg-medium`).

Follow-up (larger variant): Add full self-serve pathway CRUD (`+ New pathway` action wired to a creation endpoint). Track separately; backend work required.

Acceptance criteria:
- Visiting `/pathway` with zero items shows the empty state, not a blank grid.
- Loading state still shows the three skeletons.
- One-or-more items still renders the grid unchanged.
- Support link opens in a new tab with `rel="noreferrer"`.
- Snapshot/unit coverage added or updated for the empty branch; lint passes.
issues.notion_ticket_prompt:

Tasks:

issues.title: Click targets below 44×44 (WCAG SC 2.5.8)
issues.description: Custom IconButton component renders as <div role='button'> with 6px padding ≈ 24×24 hit area. MUI's IconButton defaults to 40×40. Both fail WCAG 2.2 Target Size Minimum (44×44). Measured: 40% of buttons on Home are under 44×44.
issues.fix: Bump base IconButton hit area to min 44×44 via padding (keeps visual icon size unchanged). Override MUI IconButton default size to 44×44 in muiOverrides.scss.
issues.dev_prompt:
Goal: Ensure every icon-only interactive element meets WCAG 2.2 SC 2.5.8 Target Size Minimum (44x44 CSS px).

Files:
- apps/called-chat/src/components/IconButton/index.tsx (currently a <div role="button"> with 6px padding at line 38)
- apps/called-chat/src/components/IconButton/index.module.scss (or sibling style file used by IconButton)
- apps/called-chat/src/components/MuiIconButtonWrapper/MuiIconButtonWrapper.tsx (MUI default is 40x40)

Changes:
1. In IconButton styles, set min-width: 44px and min-height: 44px on the root. Keep visual icon size unchanged by keeping the inner glyph at its current size; use padding/inset to inflate the hit area only.
2. In MuiIconButtonWrapper, pass an `sx` prop (or override class) so the rendered MUI IconButton enforces min-width: 44px and min-height: 44px. Do not rely on size="large".
3. Add a `compact` opt-in prop for cases where 44 truly cannot fit (toolbar density); the opt-in must require an explicit aria-label and is the exception, not the default.

Acceptance criteria:
- Computed box for every IconButton instance on Home and /chat is >= 44x44.
- Visual icon size has not grown; only padding/hit area.
- axe-core scan on Home and /chat shows zero new violations and target-size violations drop to zero.
- No regressions to existing snapshot or visual tests.

Stylelint: add `declaration-property-value-disallowed-list` discouraging raw `width: 24px`/`height: 24px` on .icon_button root selectors without an accompanying min-width/min-height.
issues.notion_ticket_prompt:
/notion-ticket
Title: Enforce 44x44 minimum hit area on IconButton and MuiIconButtonWrapper

Story:
As a touch or tremor-prone user
I want every icon button to be at least 44x44 CSS pixels
so that I can reliably tap controls without missing.

Tasks:
- Set min-width: 44px and min-height: 44px on the IconButton root in apps/called-chat/src/components/IconButton.
- Override MUI IconButton sizing in MuiIconButtonWrapper to enforce the same minimum.
- Inflate hit area via padding only; keep visual icon size unchanged.
- Add an explicit `compact` opt-in for rare toolbar-density cases that requires an aria-label.
- Spot-check Home, /chat, channel header, and profile menu in Playwright.

Acceptance Criteria:
- All IconButton instances render >= 44x44 in the DOM.
- axe-core target-size violations on Home and /chat are zero.
- Visual regression: icon glyphs remain the same size as before.
- No existing tests broken.

Team: web-isidore
Sprint: current sprint

issues.title: role='button' on <div> blocks default keyboard behaviour
issues.description: 31 instances of role='button' on divs across the app (IconButton, DropdownMenu, BottomSheet, FileDropZone, EmojiPlaceholder, multiple Feed/Pathway/Prayer cards). Need explicit onKeyDown Enter/Space handling and aren't always announced as buttons by screen readers.
issues.fix: Replace with <button type='button'> and let CSS handle appearance. For cases needing a div (drag handles), create a useButton hook that adds keyboard + ARIA correctly.
issues.dev_prompt:
Goal: Replace the 31 `role="button"` divs with real <button type="button"> so keyboard, focus, and AT semantics work natively.

Files (primary):
- apps/called-chat/src/components/IconButton/index.tsx:38
- apps/called-chat/src/components/DropdownMenu/DropdownMenu.tsx:55
- Community detail action bar call-sites: "Show QR code", "Copy invite link", "Edit Community" (see flow.json for paths).

Changes:
1. In IconButton/index.tsx, change the root element from <div role="button" tabIndex={0}> to <button type="button">. Remove manual onKeyDown Enter/Space handlers, remove tabIndex={0}, remove role="button".
2. Reset native button styles in IconButton's SCSS module: background: transparent; border: 0; padding: ...; cursor: pointer; font: inherit; color: inherit. Keep the existing visual look.
3. Do the same in DropdownMenu trigger and the three community action-bar buttons.
4. For any genuinely-non-button divs (drag handles), create a `useAriaButton` hook that adds role, tabIndex, and Enter/Space onKeyDown in one place, and refactor those call-sites to use it. Document that role="button" on a div is otherwise forbidden.
5. Add an ESLint rule (jsx-a11y/no-noninteractive-element-interactions / no-static-element-interactions already exist) and remove any inline disables that hid this pattern.

Acceptance criteria:
- grep for `role="button"` returns zero matches in apps/called-chat (or only inside useAriaButton).
- Tab order on Home and /chat is sensible; every former div-button is now focusable as a native button.
- axe-core scan reports zero "button-name" or "interactive role on non-interactive element" violations.
- Keyboard: Enter and Space both activate every refactored control.
issues.notion_ticket_prompt:
/notion-ticket
Title: Replace role=button divs with native <button> elements across web app

Story:
As a keyboard or screen-reader user
I want interactive controls to be real <button> elements
so that focus, activation, and announcements work without custom handlers.

Tasks:
- Refactor IconButton root from div+role=button to <button type="button"> and reset native styles.
- Refactor DropdownMenu trigger and community action-bar controls (QR code, Copy invite link, Edit Community).
- Introduce a useAriaButton hook for the rare div-must-stay cases (drag handles).
- Remove now-unneeded tabIndex and manual Enter/Space handlers.
- Sweep remaining role=button divs and convert each.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Zero role="button" attributes remain in apps/called-chat outside useAriaButton.
- axe-core reports zero related violations on Home and /chat.
- Enter and Space activate every refactored control.
- Visual appearance unchanged.

Team: web-isidore
Sprint: current sprint

issues.title: Theme leaks — hardcoded colours break dark mode
issues.description: EmojiSelector hardcodes 'white' background (invisible in dark mode); EventRsvpButton hardcodes 'white' text; signUpStep hardcodes brand hexes in text-shadow; PrayerContent.module.scss has 55 instances of hardcoded black/color-mix.
issues.fix: Replace with semantic aliases (var(--bg-default), var(--text-inverted-default)). Add a Stylelint rule (color-no-hex / color-named) blocking raw color literals outside variables.css.
issues.dev_prompt:
Goal: Replace hardcoded color literals with semantic theme tokens so dark mode renders correctly and contrast stays inside the design system.

Files:
- apps/called-chat/src/components/EmojiSelector/EmojiSelector.module.scss:25-26 (hardcoded `white` background)
- apps/called-chat/src/components/EventRsvpButton/EventRsvpButton.module.scss:28, 39, 50 (hardcoded `white` text)
- apps/called-chat/src/views/Auth/CalledSignUp/steps/signUpStep/signUpStep.module.scss:18, 27, 36, 44 (hardcoded brand hexes #3FC9C1, #9D57F4, #888EFF)
- apps/called-chat/src/components/Prayer/PrettyPrayer/PrayerContent.module.scss:28-29 (and ~55 instances of hardcoded black/color-mix)
- packages/called-common/src/styles/variables.css (source of semantic aliases)

Changes:
1. EmojiSelector: replace `white` with `var(--bg-default)`.
2. EventRsvpButton: replace `white` with `var(--text-inverted-default)`.
3. signUpStep: replace the three hexes with the corresponding semantic aliases in variables.css (`--brand`, `--bg-accent-1`, etc.). If a needed alias is missing, add it in variables.css rather than inlining the hex.
4. PrayerContent: replace each `black` / `color-mix(...)` with the appropriate `--text-default` / `--bg-strong` / etc. token.
5. Stylelint: enable `color-no-hex` and `color-named` rules globally, with an exception only for `packages/called-common/src/styles/variables.css`. Add `declaration-property-value-disallowed-list` to block `color-mix(` outside that file.

Acceptance criteria:
- Grep for `#[0-9a-fA-F]{3,8}` and `\b(white|black)\b` in *.scss returns zero matches outside variables.css.
- Dark-mode QA on EmojiSelector, EventRsvpButton, sign-up steps, and PrettyPrayer shows correct contrast.
- Stylelint fails CI on any new raw color literal.
- axe-core scan shows zero new contrast violations on the touched components.
issues.notion_ticket_prompt:

so that text and backgrounds remain legible and on-brand.

issues.title: Headway iframe accessibility
issues.description: Iframe is not titled, intercepts pointer events when expanded but offers no clear close affordance, and its own contrast may not match Called's design.
issues.fix: Same as NAV-06 — gate Headway behind user intent (click) and add proper iframe title attribute.
issues.dev_prompt:
Goal: Stop the Headway "What's New" iframe from intercepting pointer events and make it accessible.

Files:
- Wherever Headway is initialized in apps/called-chat (search for `HW_config`, `headway`, or `#HW_frame_cont`). Likely in the app shell / top-nav component.

Changes:
1. Defer Headway script load until the bell icon is clicked for the first time (lazy-load on user intent). Until then, do not inject `#HW_frame_cont` at all.
2. After load, ensure the host container has `pointer-events: none` by default; only the visible badge dot is `pointer-events: auto`. The expanded dropdown body gets `pointer-events: auto` only while open.
3. Add a `<iframe title="What's new in Called">` (or the wrapper equivalent) so screen-reader users know what the frame is.
4. Provide a visible close button (X) inside the dropdown with `aria-label="Close what's new"`.

Acceptance criteria:
- Top-right community cards / "Show all communities" links on Home are clickable in every state (before bell click, while dropdown open, after dropdown closes).
- Iframe has a non-empty title attribute.
- Keyboard: Tab reaches the bell, Enter opens the dropdown, Esc closes it.
- axe-core scan on Home shows zero "frame-title" violations.
- Lighthouse Performance does not regress (lazy load should help, not hurt).
issues.notion_ticket_prompt:
/notion-ticket
Title: Lazy-load Headway widget and add iframe title plus close affordance

Story:
As a user browsing the home page
I want the What's New widget to stay out of my way until I open it
so that I can click cards in the top-right and use a screen reader without surprises.

Tasks:
- Defer Headway script injection until the bell icon is clicked.
- Set pointer-events: none on the Headway container by default; auto only on the badge and open dropdown.
- Add a meaningful iframe title attribute.
- Add a visible close button with aria-label inside the dropdown.
- Verify keyboard flow (Tab, Enter, Esc) end to end.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Top-right Home cards are clickable in every Headway state.
- Iframe has a non-empty title.
- Keyboard Tab/Enter/Esc cycle works.
- axe-core frame-title violations on Home are zero.

issues.title: Destructive flows use inconsistent friction
issues.description: Community/group delete requires typing the name; channel and message delete are one-click confirms. Users learn that delete = one click, then get tripped up by typed-name flows, or worse hammer through one-click confirmations expecting friction.
issues.fix: Standardize: typed-name confirmation for community/group/channel; simple Cancel/Delete for message-level deletes.
issues.dev_prompt:
Goal: Make irreversible destructive flows consistent so users build correct muscle memory.

Files:
- apps/called-chat/src/views/Community/Communities/EntityDetails/EditDetails/DeleteEntityDialog/DeleteEntityDialog.tsx (already typed-name confirm)
- apps/called-chat/src/views/Community/Chat/ChannelMenu/ChannelMenu.tsx (channel delete, currently one-click)
- Message delete confirmation modal (search for `deleteMessage` in apps/called-chat)

Changes:
1. Extract the typed-name confirmation pattern from DeleteEntityDialog into a reusable `<ConfirmTypedNameDialog />` component (props: entityType, entityName, onConfirm).
2. Use it for channel delete in ChannelMenu.tsx, group delete, and message-of-importance delete (e.g. pinned messages, announcements). Keep one-click for low-stakes deletes (ephemeral chat message authored by current user in the last few minutes).
3. Document the threshold in a short comment at the top of the new component ("use this for any deletion that the server cannot trivially undo").

Acceptance criteria:
- Channel delete, group delete, and pinned/announcement message delete all require typing the entity name to enable the destructive button.
- The destructive button is disabled until the typed name matches exactly (case-insensitive trim).
- Existing community delete flow continues to work unchanged.
- Unit test on ConfirmTypedNameDialog covers match/no-match/whitespace cases.
- axe-core scan on the dialog reports zero violations (label association, focus trap).
issues.notion_ticket_prompt:
/notion-ticket
Title: Standardize destructive-action friction with typed-name confirmation

Story:
As an admin doing destructive actions
I want the same confirmation pattern for every irreversible delete
so that I do not blow past a one-click confirm or get surprised by a typed-name confirm.

Tasks:
- Extract ConfirmTypedNameDialog from the existing community-delete dialog.
- Apply it to channel delete, group delete, and pinned/announcement message delete.
- Keep one-click delete for low-stakes own-message removal.
- Unit-test the typed-name match logic (case, whitespace).
- Document the threshold for when to use it.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Channel, group, and important-message deletions all require typed-name confirmation.
- Destructive button is disabled until the typed name matches.
- Existing community delete flow unchanged.
- axe-core reports zero dialog violations.

issues.title: Unnamed ARIA commands (66 on /chat alone)
issues.description: /chat has 66 nodes with role='button' lacking an accessible name. Sample: profile menu's nested Sendbird avatar with role='button' has no aria-label. Screen readers announce 'button, button' — users don't know what they activate.
issues.fix: Add aria-label to every role='button' div + every native <button> that contains only an icon. Sendbird's avatar component is the biggest source — wrap or override.
issues.dev_prompt:
Goal: Give every interactive element an accessible name so screen readers stop announcing "button, button".

Files:
- apps/called-chat/src/views/Community/Chat/* (66 unnamed nodes; Sendbird avatar is the biggest source)
- apps/called-chat/src/components/ProfileMenu/* (nested avatar with role=button, no name)
- Any wrapper around the Sendbird `<Avatar>` / channel-list item

Changes:
1. Run axe-core on /chat and Home; export the list of unnamed buttons.
2. For every native <button> containing only an icon, add `aria-label` describing the action (e.g. "Reply to message from {sender}", "React with emoji", "Open channel menu").
3. For the profile menu's nested Sendbird avatar: either drop the inner role=button (preferred, see A11Y-07) or add `aria-label={`Profile of ${currentUser.nickname}`}`.
4. Where the visible text already names the action (e.g. a chat row containing the sender's name), prefer `aria-hidden="true"` on the icon and let the text be the accessible name; do not duplicate.
5. For Sendbird components we cannot patch, wrap them in a small component that injects the aria-label via prop or DOM-ref effect.

Acceptance criteria:
- axe-core reports zero "button-name" violations on /chat and Home.
- Screen-reader walkthrough of a channel announces each control with a meaningful name.
- No duplicated announcements (visible text + aria-label saying the same thing).
- Snapshot tests updated where stable text now appears in the accessible tree.
issues.notion_ticket_prompt:
/notion-ticket
Title: Add accessible names to unnamed buttons on /chat and Home

Story:
As a screen-reader user
I want every interactive control to announce what it does
so that I can navigate chat without guessing.

Tasks:
- Run axe-core on /chat and Home and export unnamed-button list.
- Add aria-label to every icon-only button (reply, react, channel menu, etc.).
- Resolve the profile-menu nested-avatar case (drop inner role=button or add label).
- Wrap Sendbird avatar components to inject aria-label where the upstream component lacks the prop.
- Avoid duplicate announcements when visible text already names the control.

Acceptance Criteria:
- axe-core button-name violations on /chat and Home are zero.
- Screen-reader walkthrough of a channel announces every control meaningfully.
- No duplicate announcements.
- Existing snapshot tests updated where needed.

issues.title: Nested interactives (60 on /chat, 5 on Home)
issues.description: Profile menu's outer <button aria-label='Open profile menu'> wraps an inner avatar with role='button'. Same pattern in chat rows: a list-item button contains an avatar button. Tab order is unpredictable; screen readers may announce both. WCAG 4.1.2.
issues.fix: Drop role='button' on the inner avatar (it's decorative inside an outer button), or flatten the structure so only the outermost element is interactive.
issues.dev_prompt:
Goal: Eliminate nested interactive elements (WCAG 4.1.2). 60 nodes on /chat, 5 on Home.

Files:
- apps/called-chat/src/components/ProfileMenu/* (outer <button aria-label="Open profile menu"> wraps Sendbird Avatar that exposes role=button)
- apps/called-chat/src/views/Community/Chat/* chat-row components with the same pattern

Changes:
1. In ProfileMenu, wrap the Sendbird Avatar in a component that sets `role={undefined}`, `tabIndex={-1}`, and `aria-hidden="true"` on the inner clickable so only the outer button is interactive.
2. Same treatment for chat-row avatars when the row itself is the interactive element (clicking the row opens DMs / profile).
3. Where the inner element really should be its own control (e.g. an explicit "View profile" inside a hover card), flatten the structure so the outer container is a non-interactive <div> and the inner avatar is the only <button>.
4. Verify Tab order is predictable: each row exposes exactly one tab stop unless intentionally multi-action.

Acceptance criteria:
- axe-core reports zero "nested-interactive" violations on /chat and Home.
- Tab through Home and /chat: each row is one stop (or N stops matching N visible controls, no hidden duplicates).
- Click handlers fire on the intended element only; no double-firing in unit/integration tests.
- Screen reader announces each row once with its label, not twice.
issues.notion_ticket_prompt:
/notion-ticket
Title: Flatten nested interactive elements in profile menu and chat rows

Story:
As a keyboard or screen-reader user
I want each row to expose only the controls it actually has
so that Tab order and announcements are predictable.

Tasks:
- Wrap Sendbird avatars in profile menu and chat rows to strip role=button and add aria-hidden.
- Confirm only the outer button is focusable per row.
- Flatten any case where the inner avatar should be the only interactive (make the outer container a plain div).
- Validate Tab order on Home and /chat.
- Re-run axe-core after changes.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Zero axe-core nested-interactive violations on /chat and Home.
- Each chat row has exactly the intended number of tab stops.
- No double-fire of click handlers in tests.
- Screen reader announces each row once.

issues.title: Missing alt text on 10 avatars (Home)
issues.description: Prayer cards, community cards, group cards render member/community avatars as without alt. Screen readers either skip them or announce the filename. WCAG 1.1.1 (critical impact per axe).
issues.fix: Add alt={name} to every rendering a member/community avatar. Or alt='' when the name is already adjacent in text (decorative).
issues.dev_prompt: Goal: Give every an appropriate alt attribute (WCAG 1.1.1). Axe flags 10 avatar images on Home as critical. Files: - apps/called-chat/src/views/Home/* prayer card, community card, and group card components rendering avatars - Shared Avatar component (likely under apps/called-chat/src/components/Avatar or packages/called-common) Changes: 1. In the shared Avatar component, require either `alt` or `decorative` prop. If `decorative={true}`, render `alt=""`. Otherwise render `alt={name}`. 2. In prayer cards, community cards, group cards on Home, pass `alt={name}` for each avatar. 3. Where the avatar sits inline next to the same name (so the name is already in the accessible text), pass `decorative` so it renders `alt=""` and screen readers don't announce the name twice. 4. Add a unit test on Avatar that fails the build if neither prop is provided. Acceptance criteria: - axe-core reports zero "image-alt" violations on Home. - Every rendered by Avatar has either a non-empty alt or alt="". - Snapshot tests updated. - TypeScript prop type for Avatar makes one of `alt` or `decorative` required (discriminated union or conditional types).
issues.notion_ticket_prompt:
/notion-ticket
Title: Require alt or decorative on every Avatar and fix Home prayer/community/group cards

Story:
As a screen-reader user
I want avatar images to either name the person or be silently decorative
so that I do not hear filenames or get nothing at all.

Tasks:
- Make Avatar require either alt or decorative via TypeScript discriminated union.
- Pass alt={name} on prayer cards, community cards, and group cards on Home.
- Pass decorative where the name is already adjacent in text.
- Add a unit test that fails when neither prop is provided.
- Re-run axe-core on Home.

Acceptance Criteria:
- axe-core image-alt violations on Home are zero.
- Every Avatar img has alt or alt="".
- TypeScript prevents building without one of the two props.
- Snapshot tests updated.

issues.title: Colour contrast failures (13 on Home, 10 on /chat)
issues.description: Multiple text-on-bg combinations below WCAG AA 4.5:1. Sample: '_empty_state_title' ('No upcoming events') fails. Many --text-weak uses fail on --bg-default in both themes.
issues.fix: Audit semantic tokens. Either bump --text-weak luminance to meet AA, or rename it to --text-decorative and stop using it for primary copy. Add an automated contrast check in CI.
issues.dev_prompt:
Goal: Bring all text into WCAG AA contrast (4.5:1 normal, 3:1 large). Axe flags 13 nodes on Home and 10 on /chat; sample is the "No upcoming events" empty-state title.

Files:
- packages/called-common/src/styles/variables.css (semantic token definitions; `--text-weak` is the prime suspect)
- apps/called-chat/src/components/EmptyState/* or wherever `._empty_state_title_g31hx_68` is defined
- Any component currently using `--text-weak` for primary copy

Changes:
1. Measure current contrast of `--text-weak` on `--bg-default` and `--bg-medium` in both light and dark themes. Document the readings in a comment in variables.css.
2. Bump `--text-weak` luminance until it clears 4.5:1 on `--bg-default` in both themes. If that breaks intended hierarchy, introduce `--text-muted` for true secondary copy (3:1 on large text only) and migrate non-primary usages there.
3. For the empty-state title specifically, switch from `--text-weak` to `--text-default` since it's the primary content of an empty state.
4. Audit other primary uses of `--text-weak` (run a grep) and migrate where the copy is informational rather than truly secondary.

Acceptance criteria:
- axe-core contrast violations on Home drop from 13 to 0; on /chat from 10 to 0.
- Visual diff of light and dark themes shows acceptable hierarchy; no "everything is the same gray" regression.
- Variables.css documents measured contrast ratios in a comment block.
- Unit/visual snapshots updated where needed.
issues.notion_ticket_prompt:

- Snapshots updated.

issues.title: Sub-44 click targets (40% on Home, 'Remove prayer' is 104×16)
issues.description: Measured on Home: 17 of 42 interactive elements are under 44×44. Worst: 'Remove prayer request' at 104×16. Open notifications 36×36, Open profile menu 40×40, prayer filter tabs 84×28.
issues.fix: Bump button base min-height: 44px and min-width: 44px (use padding to inflate hit area while keeping visual size). Update IconButton component to enforce 44×44 minimum.
issues.dev_prompt:
Goal: Bring 17 of 42 Home interactive elements up to 44x44 minimum (WCAG 2.5.8). Worst offenders: "Remove prayer request" (104x16), "Open notifications" (36x36), "Open profile menu" (40x40), prayer filter tabs (84x28).

Files:
- apps/called-chat/src/views/Home/* prayer card (Remove prayer request control)
- apps/called-chat/src/components/TopNav/* (Open notifications, Open profile menu)
- apps/called-chat/src/views/Home/PrayerFilters/* (filter tabs)

Changes:
1. "Remove prayer request": keep the visual chip at 104x16 if needed, but pad the hit area (padding: 14px 0) and set min-height: 44px. Use ::before with negative inset to expand the click region without affecting layout if padding shifts neighbors.
2. Open notifications and Open profile menu: set min-width: 44px / min-height: 44px on the IconButton root (depends on A11Y-01 fix landing too).
3. Prayer filter tabs: bump min-height to 44px; keep current visual height by adjusting internal padding. If the tab strip can't grow vertically, increase touch padding inline using a transparent inset wrapper.
4. Add a Playwright check that measures bounding rects for the four named controls and fails if any side < 44.

Acceptance criteria:
- Every measured Home interactive (all 42) is >= 44x44 in computed box or hit area.
- axe-core target-size violations on Home drop to zero.
- Visual design of cards/filters/topbar is preserved (no obvious layout shift).
- Playwright dimension assertion runs in CI for the four named controls.
- No regression on existing Home Vitest/Playwright tests.
issues.notion_ticket_prompt:

issues.title: Naming overload — 'Group' means two things
issues.description: A top-level Community can be named 'Group Testing' (test data); the left-nav 'Groups' goes to a list of sub-groups; the 'Your Groups' card on Home also shows sub-groups. Admins frequently confuse the levels.
issues.fix: Rename left-nav 'Groups' → 'Sub-groups' or 'My ministries'. Add a tooltip 'Groups live inside Communities' on first visit.
issues.dev_prompt:
Goal (small variant): Reduce the Community vs sub-group naming collision by relabeling the left-nav and bottom-nav entry currently titled 'Groups' to 'Sub-groups'. Route and code identifiers stay the same; this is a display-only change.

Files:
- `apps/called-chat/src/utils/route.ts` line 14: change `groups: { route: 'groups', title: 'Groups' }` to `groups: { route: 'groups', title: 'Sub-groups' }`.
- Verify all consumers via Grep for `calledChatRoutes.groups.title` -- consumers are `apps/called-chat/src/components/ActionBar/ActionBar.tsx` and `apps/called-chat/src/components/BottomNavigation/BottomNavigation.tsx`. They already read the title from the route object so no extra changes are needed.
- Update any unit tests asserting the literal string 'Groups' (e.g. snapshot or text matches in `ActionBar`, `BottomNavigation`, `ViewRouteWrapper` tests).

Follow-up (larger variant): Add a one-time onboarding tooltip on first visit: 'Groups live inside Communities.' Track separately.

Acceptance criteria:

- Page title / breadcrumb derived from `calledChatRoutes.groups.title` updates accordingly.
- Route URL stays `/groups`.
- No analytics keys break.
- Tests at repo root pass; lint clean.

issues.title: Auto-created Women's Group + Bible Study on new community
issues.description: Creating a brand-new community silently inserts a 'Women's Group' default sub-group and a 'Bible Study' upcoming event. Not disclosed in the wizard. Admins find them, get confused about origin, sometimes delete + recreate trying to understand.
issues.fix: Either remove the auto-creation, or surface a checkbox in step 2: 'Start with sample group + event? (Recommended for new admins)'. Default checked for now.
issues.dev_prompt:
Goal: Stop surprising new admins with a 'Women's Group' sub-group and a 'Bible Study' event that appear silently after community creation. Surface a disclosure in the wizard so admins know sample content is coming and can opt out (or at least are not confused later).

File: `apps/called-chat/src/views/Community/CommunityCreation/Steps/CommunityDetailsStep.tsx`.

Change: Below the existing size slider, add a small static disclosure block (no form control change needed for the minimal variant): a one-line label like 'We will add a sample sub-group and upcoming event so you can see how everything fits together. You can remove them anytime.' Style with existing semantic tokens (`--text-weak`, `--bg-medium`) in `CommunityDetailsStep.module.scss`. Do not introduce a new prop or change the create payload.

Follow-up (larger variant): Add an optional 'Start with sample group + event' checkbox wired through `CreateCommunityState` and a new flag on the create-entity request so admins can opt out. Requires backend coordination; track separately.

Acceptance criteria:
- Disclosure copy renders on step 2 of the Create Community wizard, below the size slider.
- Copy is plain language, no jargon, no Tailwind, uses semantic color tokens.
- No change to the create-entity API call.
- Wizard still completes successfully and the existing onboarding analytics fire unchanged.
- Lint and unit tests at repo root pass.

issues.title: Settings external links look identical to internal
issues.description: Settings sidebar: Give feedback / Support / About called / Privacy policy / Terms of service / Release notes / Get the app all open in new tabs but look identical to internal links (Edit Profile, Manage communities, Blocked users). Users don't know which open new tabs.
issues.fix: Add a small external-link icon on those rows, or move them into a secondary section under a divider labelled 'Help & resources'.
issues.dev_prompt:

Already imported elsewhere in the codebase (`NewsControls.tsx`, `VideoFormComponent.tsx`). On mobile the chevron currently appears for internal rows -- replace the chevron with `ArrowSquareOut` on the external rows so the affordance is consistent. Add an `external_icon` class in `ProfileActions.module.scss` sized via `font-size: 16px` and colored with `var(--icon-default)`.
